title: Finite Cauchy-Riemann Shadow Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Cauchy-Riemann bad derivative real-part QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact real-pair replay for f(z)=z^2 at z=1+2i computes f'(z)=2+4i, while a malformed derivative-real claim asserts 3, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional complex-analysis rows only when they add distinct real-pair, polynomial-partial, denominator, branch, contour, or theorem-horizon pressure; keep general holomorphicity and Cauchy-Riemann theorem schemas in future Lean lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed complex input, real-pair square, component polynomials, exact partial derivatives, Cauchy-Riemann equalities, and complex derivative as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false derivative real-part claim through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact derivative component.
Expose the pack through complex-analysis, real-analysis, derivative-shadow, polynomial-replay, and complex-real-pair learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep general holomorphicity, differentiability-from-limits, Cauchy-Riemann equivalence theorems, Cauchy's theorem, residues, and analytic continuation marked as Lean-horizon work until formal proof routes exist.
